rag_rules: Fix unresolved property references by ensuring all property definitions and constants are properly declared in the AADL model or its dependencies before use in AGREE contracts.
Resolve type errors in AGREE expressions by ensuring all identifiers refer to visible AADL features or properly declared helpers with matching types.
When AGREE reports 'named thing must be an expression with a type', trace and correct the first unresolved identifier or invalid helper declaration on the same line.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, only reference visible AADL features or helpers declared within the same annex to avoid type resolution errors.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in const and eq declarations to prevent resolution failures.
Place AGREE annexes in appropriate component type or implementation scopes based on the visibility and scope of referenced elements.
Avoid referencing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE that are not present in the analyzed model's configured dependencies.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within valid component type or implementation scopes.
Guarantee expressions must have well-defined types; comparisons are bool, integer arithmetic is int, real arithmetic is real.
Constants and equation helpers in AGREE must be declared with correct scalar types (int, real, bool) to match expected usage.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce property references or identifiers in AGREE that are not declared or visible in the AADL model or its dependencies.
Do not write AGREE guarantees or equations that reference unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations without fixing the root cause.
Do not place AGREE annexes in scopes where referenced AADL features or properties are not visible or accessible.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
